decisions.support_rationale: Claim text exactly matches evidence item 1, establishing the proposition directly.

Evidence item 3 explicitly states the stack is not the root cause, which directly supports the proposition to not blame the stack for the failure.

While evidence shows a same-stack project failed (item 1) and the stack was not the root cause (item 3), the claim to avoid the stack due to failure contradicts the finding that the stack was not the cause of the failure.

Evidence 4 explicitly states the fast rollout failed, directly contradicting the proposition to choose a fast ops-heavy plan, while other evidence suggests constraints (low capacity, tight budget) that further undermine this choice.

Claim 5 is an exact textual match to Evidence 4, establishing the exception that the fast rollout failed.

While no evidence explicitly names the 'low_ops_incremental_plan', the combination of low ops capacity (Evidence 2), tight budget (Evidence 3), and the failure of the fast rollout (Evidence 4) logically supports the direction of choosing a lower-ops, incremental approach, though the specific label is an inference.
